Details: Two distinct ECL-to-Java Spring Boot conversion implementations with fundamentally different purposes and complexity levels. Constants_Converter provides a simple REST API for configuration constants while CleanDLN_Converter implements complex data cleaning logic with full service architecture.

Both outputs convert ECL modules to Java Spring Boot but serve completely different functional purposes. Constants_Converter handles static configuration values (NOT_FOUND_STATUS and MAX_JOIN_LIMIT) while CleanDLN_Converter implements complex Driver License Number cleaning logic with regex patterns and conditional processing. The semantic overlap is limited to the shared Spring Boot framework usage and ECL conversion context.

Both follow Spring Boot architectural patterns but with different complexity levels. Constants_Converter uses simple structure: Constants class (lines 10-16) + Controller (lines 18-28) + Response POJO (lines 34-50) + Application class (lines 60-67). CleanDLN_Converter employs full service architecture: Constants (lines 5-9) + Patterns (lines 14-17) + Request/Response POJOs (lines 22-45) + Service layer (lines 50-95) + Controller (lines 100-115) + Exception handling (lines 120-130) + Application class (lines 135-140). Both use @RestController and @SpringBootApplication annotations but CleanDLN_Converter adds @Service and comprehensive error handling.
